ledger_sites.exemption_note: loadActionRequiredHandoffs — the awaited chain carries .order(created_at).limit(50) two lines below the statement window
multi-part group lookup — .limit(50) DESC on the chain below the heuristic window (deliberate newest-first window, PR #6191)
selectUnactionedAdvisories — caller-bounded .limit(limit), default 20
update-returning select — bounded by the retarget UPDATE result set
getIssuesPerRunTrend — caller-bounded .limit(k), default 5 (last-K runs)
per-run stage rows — bounded by the fixed stage count of a single convergence run
ROWCAP-CANONICAL-001: deliberately newest-first ordered so the cap can only drop the STALEST sessions; all downstream derivations are fresh()-gated (documented at the site)
eva_scheduler_heartbeat — one liveness row per scheduler instance (operationally 1)
pending+unfulfilled worker_spawn_requests — operationally tiny set with expiry window (mirrors fleet-dashboard.cjs:200)
oldest-row probe — .order(created_at asc).limit(1) applied on the wrapped chain
sd_phase_handoffs within the RECENT_MIN window — small window-bounded transition set (documented at the site, SD-REFILL-00C7I5BY)
.in(recentSdIds)-bounded near-terminal intersect — bounded by the windowed handoff id list
.in(keys)-bounded existence probe — bounded by the parsed proposal-key list
getReadReceipts — caller-bounded .limit(limit), default 50, newest-first
getFleetPresence — caller-bounded .order(heartbeat_at desc).limit(limit), default 60 (newest-first so live sessions are never truncated out)
.in(ventureIds)-bounded ground-truth read — bounded by the venture-id list derived from the paginated SD read above
update-returning select — idempotency-claim UPDATE on a single row (eq id)
head-only estimated count — zero rows fetched (no truncation surface); estimate is the deliberate design for row-growth trending

getViolations — caller-bounded .limit(limit), default 100
getViolations — caller-bounded .limit(limit), default 100

active aegis_rules registry — governed enumerated rule set (cached loader)

per-SD chairman override-request history — small per-sd_id set

NOT paginated: per-objective key_results are tiny; same mock constraint as the scorecard read; warnIfCapTruncated applied at assignment

NOT paginated: vision-portfolio-scorecard.test.js mock resolves the chain at .order() (no .range()); declared .limit(5000) sample is server-clamped to 1000 — warnIfCapTruncated fires at that signature

NOT paginated: harness-backlog-parser.test.js stub resolves the chain at .order() (no .range()); warnIfCapTruncated applied after the error-check. The BADGE COUNT no longer uses this fetch's rows.length — a separate exact head-count gauge (renderCount) supplies it (adversarial-review fix)

checkDependenciesResolved — .or(sd_key.in/id.in) lookup with .limit(depKeys.length*2), bounded by the parsed dependency list
.in(sdUUIDs) where the parent SD read caps at .limit(15) — at most 15 SDs x a handful of KR alignments each

.in(ventureIds) — one ventures row per distinct venture_id on the filtered SD list

NOT paginated (FR-6 batch 3): the chainable stubs in worker-checkin-fleet-identity/-callsign-collision-fix/-metadata-race tests have no .order()/.range() (same constraint as stale-session-sweep.cjs:1015); the live-5-min-heartbeat set is operationally tiny, and a truncated used-set's worst case (duplicate callsign pick) is healed by the 5-min dedupeAssignedCallsigns cron pass by design
.in()-bounded lookup — exactly two enumerated system_settings keys (FLEET_STAND_DOWN, HARD_HALT_STATUS)
.limit(QF_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=100) with deterministic .order(created_at) — a designed candidate window, not a silent cap (heuristic only sees literal .limit(N))
.in('sd_key', keys)-bounded — keys come from the merged self-claim candidate pool (~45 max across the 5 bounded windows)
.limit(DRAFT_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=10) with deterministic .order(created_at ASC) — the oldest-N draft window is bounded by design (fetchDraftCandidates)
.limit(DRAFT_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=10) with deterministic .order(created_at DESC) — the newest-N draft window is bounded by design (fetchNewestDraftCandidates)
.limit(DRAFT_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=10) with deterministic .order(priority, created_at) — the fleet_critical window is bounded by design (fetchFleetCriticalCandidates)
.limit(DRAFT_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=10) with deterministic .order(created_at) — the ranked-candidates window is bounded by design (fetchRankedCandidates)
.limit(STRANDED_CANDIDATE_LIMIT=5) with deterministic .order(updated_at ASC) — oldest-stranded recovery window, bounded by design (recoverStrandedFinal)
